question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks with 100 repeated limit parameters (limit=100&limit=100&...×100) where buildAndValidateFilters() processes all of them and findLast() selects the last one, causing unnecessary filter processing overhead that, when multiplied across thousands of concurrent requests, exhausts Node.js CPU?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractOrderFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send 100 repeated order=asc parameters causing extractOrderFromFilters() to call findLast() over a 100-element array on every request, and when multiplied across thousands of concurrent requests, cause measurable Node.js CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?timestamp=gte:1&timestamp=lte:2 with a 1-nanosecond range to force the DB to execute a highly selective range scan that still requires index traversal, and by sending thousands of such requests with different 1-nanosecond windows exhaust DB index read I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block number equal to 9223372036854775807 (maxLong) to getByHashOrNumber(), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute a query for a non-existent block, and by sending thousands of such 404-generating requests exhaust DB connection pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBlocks() maps every returned row through new BlockViewModel(model) by requesting limit=100 blocks, causing 100 BlockViewModel constructor calls per request including nsToSecNs() and toHexString() operations, and by sending thousands of concurrent requests exhaust Node.js CPU through response serialization overhead?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?block.number=gte:0&order=asc&limit=100 and then follow the next link repeatedly in an automated loop, causing the server to execute a continuous stream of sequential DB queries that collectively scan the entire block history and exhaust DB I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send 50 block.number filters and 50 timestamp filters (total 100, at maxRepeatedQueryParameters) causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a 100-condition WHERE clause that forces the DB to evaluate both index and timestamp predicates on every candidate row, causing CPU exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a 0x-prefixed 96-character hex hash to getByHashOrNumber(), where validateHashOrNumber() strips the 0x prefix leaving a 96-char hash used in a LIKE query, and by sending thousands of such requests with different hash prefixes exhaust DB hash index I/O?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?timestamp=lte:9999999999999999999 with only an upper-bound timestamp filter and no lower bound, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to scan from the beginning of the table up to the maximum timestamp, performing a near-full table scan on every request?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send block.number=gte:0 combined with timestamp=gte:0 to cause extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a WHERE clause with both conditions, where the DB must evaluate both predicates and cannot use a single covering index, resulting in higher per-query cost that enables DoS through sustained requests?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request-level caching in getBlocks() by sending identical requests (e.g., GET /api/v1/blocks?limit=100) at high frequency, causing each request to independently hit the DB without cache benefit and exhausting DB connection pool resources?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a block number of 0 (zero, allowed by isPositiveLong(hashOrNumber, true)) to getByHashOrNumber() causing a DB query for index=0, and by sending thousands of such requests exhaust DB connection pool resources through repeated queries for a likely non-existent genesis block?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the limit fallback logic (limit.value > maxLimit ? defaultLimit : limit.value) where sending limit=maxLimit+1 returns defaultLimit rows but still executes a full DB query, enabling an attacker to send high-frequency requests that each consume DB resources while appearing to request fewer rows?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?block.number=gte:0&block.number=lte:0 to force a single-block range query, and by sending thousands of such requests with different block.number=eq:N values exhaust DB index lookup resources through high-frequency point queries?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send 100 block.number=gte:0 filters (at maxRepeatedQueryParameters limit) causing buildWhereSqlStatement() in RecordFileService to generate a WHERE clause with 100 identical conditions and 100 bound parameters, causing the DB to allocate excessive memory for parameter binding and plan caching?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?order=asc with no other filters, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute an ascending full-table scan ordered by consensus_end with limit=defaultLimit=25, and by sending this at high frequency exhaust DB sort buffer memory?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit generateNextLink() using last(blocks).index to construct a next link, where the attacker follows the link chain at maximum speed to continuously paginate through all blocks, causing sustained sequential DB reads that exhaust I/O bandwidth?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a hash that is all 'f' characters (ffffffff...ffff, 64 chars) to getByHashOrNumber(), causing the LIKE query to scan from the lexicographically largest possible prefix to the end of the hash index, potentially scanning many index pages before returning no results?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/blockController.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /api/v1/blocks?timestamp=gte:0&limit=100 with no upper timestamp bound, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to scan from timestamp 0 forward and return 100 rows, and by sending this request from many concurrent connections simultaneously exhaust the DB connection pool?
